strategy: Enter the fight on time with your team. Joining late after Round 2 triggers Athena Battlesight, Witch's House Call (992-1,132), and Orthrus dumped onto the latecomer.
Keep at least some damage landing on Hercules every single round. As long as he takes damage he cannot end the round at full health, which shuts off his protected +100% Balanceblade cheat.

The cleanest way to keep chip damage flowing is a low-damage off-school damage-over-time each cycle: Fire Elf, Frostbite, Poison, Death Bat, Black Widow, or Steel Giant. The DoT ticks while your team blades safely.

In a group, assign one wizard as the consistent attacker who pokes Hercules each round, a hitter who charges multi-hits like Bugs, Raging Bull, or Mystic Colossus, and a healer running Satyr with Life mastery.
Wear Tower or Dream Shields and pack healing. Hercules has 35% universal pierce, so raw shields alone leak damage. Shadow spells like Shrike add pierce for the final hit.

Note his heal cheat when he is not at full health: a flat 1,518 Blessing that ignores Infection and Sanctuary and is not removed by Entangle. Do not waste rounds trying to block it; just out-damage it and burst him down.
cheatNotes: If you enter after Round 2, Hercules immediately loads the latecomer with Athena Battlesight, a Witch's House Call hit for 992-1,132, and an Orthrus. Load in with your team from the start.
Any round he ends at full health, he gives himself a protected +100% Balanceblade. Keep a hit or a damage-over-time landing on him every round so he is never at full health at round's end.

When he is below full health he casts a special 1,518 Blessing (10% of his health) that ignores Infection, Sanctuary, and Entangle. You cannot stop this heal, so plan for extra damage rather than trying to block it.
